question_title: Will the April 2026 Android Security Bulletin list at least one vulnerability rated “Critical” in Android Framework or System?
summary: The question asks whether the April 2026 Android Security Bulletin will include at least one Critical-severity vulnerability in the Framework or System sections. I checked 15 months of Android Security Bulletins (2024-2026). Overall, 8/15 (53%) had at least one Critical in Framework/System. But the recent trend is stronger: in 2025-2026, 8/12 months (67%) had Critical vulnerabilities, and the last 5 consecutive months with patches (Aug 2025 through Mar 2026) ALL had Critical-rated issues, predominantly in the System component (often Bluetooth RCE). April 2025 also had a Critical. However, some months (Feb, May, Jun 2025) and some 2024 months did not have Critical in these sections, showing it's not guaranteed. Weighing the strong recent streak against the historical minority of months without Critical, I estimate ~79% probability.

factors.description: Overall base rate: 53% of bulletins checked (8/15) had at least one Critical in Framework/System
Recent trend (2025-2026): 67-73% of months had Critical vulnerabilities in Framework/System, showing higher recent frequency
Last 5 consecutive months with patches (Aug 2025 through Mar 2026) ALL had Critical vulnerabilities - strong recent streak
System component Bluetooth stack is a persistent source of Critical RCE bugs - structural pattern likely to continue
April 2024 had NO Critical in Framework/System, showing April is not guaranteed
Some months (May, Jun, Feb 2025) had no Critical at all - approximately 1 in 3 months lack Critical in these sections

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.79 (79% probability YES)**

**Key findings from analyzing 15 months of Android Security Bulletins:**

| Period | Base Rate (Critical in Framework/System) |
|--------|----------------------------------------|
| All months checked (2024-2026) | 8/15 = 53% |
| 2025-2026 only | 8/12 = 67% |
| 2025-2026 excl. Jul (no patches) | 8/11 = 73% |
| Last 5 months with patches | 5/5 = 100% |

**For YES (~79%):**
- Strong recent streak: every month from Aug 2025 through Mar 2026 had Critical vulnerabilities in System or Framework
- The System component's Bluetooth stack is a structural, recurring source of Critical RCE bugs
- April 2025 specifically had a Critical vulnerability (CVE-2025-26416)

**For NO (~21%):**
- Some months (Feb, May, Jun 2025; Apr, Jun, Dec 2024) had no Critical in these sections
- Roughly 1 in 3-4 recent months lacked Critical severity in Framework/System
- April 2024 specifically had no Critical, showing the month isn't guaranteed

[Android Security and Update Bulletins | Android Open Source Project](https://source.android.com/docs/security/bulletin)
[Android Security Bulletin December 2024 | Android Open Source Project](https://source.android.com/docs/security/bulletin/2024-12-01)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the April 2026 Android Security Bulletin, includes at least one entry in the Android “Framework” or “System” sections with severity rated “Critical.” This question resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is the Android Security Bulletins site at https://source.android.com/docs/security/bulletin
fine_print: Use the severity labeling as shown in the bulletin tables. Count only Framework or System (exclude Kernel, vendor components, and Pixel/Qualcomm-specific sections). Candidate Sources: Direct monthly bulletin pages (when published) under https://source.android.com/docs/security/bulletin/2026 | Android Security Bulletin RSS or announcements (backup navigation) https://source.android.com/docs/security/bulletin
background_info:
This forecasts near-term Android platform severity, relevant for patch management and mobile security risk.
